summary.intent: Run QA verification on the CHA-17 implementation (crm.matters.* RPC filtering/pagination methods) to validate quality gates before marking the issue complete.
summary.outcome: QA skill was invoked and partial verification was performed, with at least one file modified (crm_service.rs), but the transcript is truncated and the final quality gate results are not visible.

repo: The project uses a pinned nightly Rust toolchain with strict quality gates: fmt, clippy -D warnings, and full test suite (496 tests).
CRM list methods follow a consistent pattern: accept a serde_json::Value params argument, parse filters internally, and delegate to a store method with explicit filter parameters.
Dynamic SQL with WHERE clauses and LIMIT/OFFSET is the established pattern for filtered queries in the SQLite store.
New database indexes require a migration file; phase and practice_area columns on matters needed indexes for filtered query performance.
The store trait uses a DRY pattern where simpler methods (list_matters, list_matters_by_contact) delegate to the more general list_matters_filtered.

code.finding: Store trait extended with list_matters_filtered(contact_id, status, phase, practice_area, search, offset, limit) signature.
Dynamic SQL construction with conditional WHERE clauses; LIKE used for case-insensitive search on title and description fields. 9 new tests added.
In-memory store implements matching filter logic for test parity. 5 new tests added.
list_matters() signature changed to list_matters(params: Value) to match all other CRM list methods.
LiveCrmService parses filter params from JSON Value and routes to filtered store method. Modified during QA run.
RPC handler passes ctx.params through to list_matters rather than ignoring them.

workflow: The orchestration pipeline uses Linear comments as a persistent workpad for tracking state across runs, appending on rework rather than overwriting.
QA runs are a distinct lifecycle stage that can trigger file modifications (not just read-only verification).
The agent checks for existing branches and PRs before creating new ones to avoid duplicates on rework runs.
Port detection and app connectivity checks are part of the QA preamble to determine if a live app is available for integration testing.

friction: The transcript is truncated — clippy and test suite results are not visible, so it's unclear whether QA fully passed.
QA required a code fix in crm_service.rs during the QA stage itself (run 2), indicating the prior implementation had a remaining issue.
Multiple tool calls were needed just to detect the running app and its ports, suggesting the local dev environment setup is not straightforward.
The Linear comment 'Hey what's going here I don't see planning' indicates a prior communication gap around the planning phase visibility.
open_items: Status transition validation (e.g., archived → open) is explicitly deferred.
FTS5 full-text search deferred in favor of LIKE.
UUID validation on IDs is a pre-existing gap not addressed.
crm.contacts.list filtering has the same gap and needs a separate issue.
3 code quality issues were identified during review but deferred as trivial or pre-existing.
